# Module 15 — sample data

This module ships a **small, purpose-built synthetic NTFS disk image** plus the
**`$MFT`** extracted from it. Both are generated by the included
[`build-image.sh`](build-image.sh), so the evidence is 100% transparent and
reproducible — you can regenerate every byte and verify there is nothing hidden.

> **Be honest about your evidence (a DFIR lesson in itself).** This is **not** a
> dump of a real victim machine and contains **no real malware** — `coreupdater.exe`
> here is a 77-byte text stub with an `MZ` header, not an executable. The image
> re-uses the running characters of the lab's narrative — host **`DESKTOP-SDN1RPT`**,
> user **`mortysmith`**, malware name **`coreupdater.exe`** — drawn from the public
> **DFIR Madness "Stolen Szechuan Sauce" (Case 001)** teaching dataset, so it threads
> with Modules 1-4. The *filesystem-level ground truth* (timestomping, deletions,
> partition layout) is constructed by us so the exercises have exact, checkable answers.

## Files

| File | Size | What it is |
|---|---|---|
| `disk-DESKTOP-SDN1RPT.raw` | 10 MiB | Raw `dd`-style disk image: an MBR partition table with **one NTFS partition starting at sector 2048**. Mostly zero (compresses to a tiny git blob). |
| `MFT` | ~82 KB | The **`$MFT`** carved out of that image with `icat -o 2048 … 0` — the input for the MFTECmd exercise. |
| `build-image.sh` | — | The generator. Run `sudo bash build-image.sh` on a Linux host (needs `parted`, `ntfs-3g`, `attr`, `python3`, Docker) to rebuild both artifacts. |
| `get-data.sh` | — | Optional: guidance + fetch for practising on a **real** forensic image (DFIR Madness Case 001). Offline-safe. |

## Ground truth baked into the image (so the exercises have answers)

| Path | MFT entry | State | Teaching point |
|---|---|---|---|
| `Windows/Temp/coreupdater.exe` | 79 | allocated | **Timestomped** C2 implant: `$SI` Created backdated to **2019-03-15 12:00:00.000** (whole-second), but `$FN` Created is the real **2026-06-15 09:12:33.456** → `$SI` < `$FN`. |
| `Windows/System32/win32k.sys` | 75 | allocated | A genuinely **old** OS file: `$SI` **and** `$FN` both 2019, consistent, sub-second precision → looks old **and is** old (the control, *not* stomped). |
| `Users/mortysmith/AppData/Local/Temp/update.ps1` | 80 | **deleted** | Stage-2 PowerShell downloader; resident → recoverable with `icat`. |
| `Users/mortysmith/Downloads/loot.zip` | 81 | **deleted** | ~13 KB exfil archive; **non-resident** (spans clusters) → real cluster carving with `icat -r`. |
| `Users/mortysmith/Documents/Q3_recipes.xlsx`, `…/Desktop/notes.txt`, `Windows/System32/cmd.exe` | 77, 78, 76 | allocated | Benign baseline for contrast. |

All times are **UTC**. The intrusion window is **2026-06-15 ~09:10–09:20**; the
`$SI` MFT-change times cluster on the **collection day** the image was built.

## Provenance & licence

- **Synthetic image + `$MFT`:** authored by this lab specifically for Module 15;
  released under the same licence as the repository. Free to use, modify, redistribute
  for training. Contains no third-party data and no malware.
- **Narrative/character names** (`DESKTOP-SDN1RPT`, `mortysmith`, `coreupdater.exe`):
  inspired by **DFIR Madness — "The Stolen Szechuan Sauce" (Case 001)**,
  <https://dfirmadness.com/the-stolen-szechuan-sauce/>, a publicly published intrusion
  dataset for education. Use the real dataset for **defensive/training forensics only**
  and respect the author's terms. `get-data.sh` points you to it for practice on a
  full, real disk image.
